subject: Physics
title: Volta Invents the Battery
description: A fast breakdown of Volta Invents the Battery for builders.

hook.text: In 1800, Alessandro Volta stacked zinc and copper discs with brine-soaked cloth and created the first battery.

buildup.text: Volta was inspired by Galvani's twitching frog legs. He suspected chemistry, not animal spirits, was the cause.

discovery.text: His 'Voltaic pile' produced a steady electric current for the first time—something static electricity could never do.

twist.text: Napoleon was so impressed he gave Volta a gold medal and made him a count.

climax.text: The battery made all electrical experiments possible. The unit of voltage—the volt—is named after him.

punchline.text: Zinc, copper, and saltwater. The first steady current. The battery was born.

quiz.question: What inspired Volta to create the battery?
options: Galvani's experiments with frog legs and electricity
